clinical_history: 73-year-old woman presenting with dyspnea.  What is the most likely diagnosis?
diagnosis: c)  Findings here are highly suggestive of moderately severe Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), including 1) relatively low voltage; 2) incomplete right bundle branch block (rsr' in V1 - V3); 3) borderline peaked P waves (possible right atrial overload); 4) poor initial R wave progression; and 5) atrial premature beats.
